content.problemStatement:
For a permutation PPP, define f(P,K)f(P, K)f(P,K) in the following way:


Start with an empty set of pairs SSS.
You can do the following action repeatedly as many times as you want:

Choose 222 integers 1≤u<v≤N1 \le u < v \le N1≤u<v≤N
Swap PuP_uPu​ and PvP_vPv​
Add (u,v)(u, v)(u,v) to our set SSS


When PPP is sorted, we define the scorescorescore to be ∑(u+v+K)\sum(u + v + K)∑(u+v+K) for distinct (u,v)(u, v)(u,v) in the set SSS
f(P)f(P)f(P) is the minimum possible score to obtain a sorted permutation.
You are given integers NNN and KKK. Find the sum of f(P)f(P)f(P) over all permutations of the integers [1,N][1, N][1,N]. Since the answer may be large, print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of input contains 222 integers - NNN and KKK.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of f(P)f(P)f(P) modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 :  There are 222 permutations - [1,2][1, 2][1,2] is already sorted hence 000 score needed; for [2,1][2, 1][2,1] we swap P1P_1P1​ and P2P_2P2​ for a cost of 333.
